title: To His Friend To Avoid Contention Of Words.
content:
Words beget anger; anger brings forth blows;
    Blows make of dearest friends immortal foes.
    For which prevention, sociate, let there be
    Betwixt us two no more logomachy.
    Far better 'twere for either to be mute,
    Than for to murder friendship by dispute.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To His Friend To Avoid Contention Of Words.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
